issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, causing them to overlap or blur together. This significantly impairs readability for all users, especially those with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 199: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Line-height below 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: A line-height of 1 is too cramped for paragraph text, making it difficult for users to track lines. WCAG recommends a line-height of at least 1.5 for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.explanation: Removing underlines from links makes them indistinguishable from surrounding text except by color. This fails to meet accessibility standards for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global focus-visible style removes the default browser outline without providing a visible alternative. This makes it impossible for keyboard users to know which element is currently focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the focus outline for non-keyboard interactions. While intended to reduce visual noise, it can hide focus from users who use a mix of input methods or certain assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: outline: 2px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The current implementation of the visually-hidden utility uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible on the screen. This defeats the purpose of a screen-reader-only class.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative word-spacing value of -0.2em. This reduces the space between words, making text harder to distinguish and read for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative letter-spacing value of -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters can cause letters to overlap or blur together, significantly hindering readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 167: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have a line-height of 1. This fails to meet the minimum requirement of 1.5 for body text, which is necessary to ensure sufficient vertical space between lines for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from all anchor elements globally. Without underlines, links within blocks of text rely solely on color to be identified, which is a failure for users with color vision deficiency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none without defining a visible replacement style like a solid outline or box-shadow. This prevents keyboard users from knowing which element currently has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually

issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu initialization script registers triggers and panels but does not explicitly set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the buttons. Assistive technologies may not correctly announce the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open(), .close(), and .toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2200: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`. Line 2220: ensure toggle calls the updated open/close.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function changes the active billing period but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle button(s). Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In setPeriod(), after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add code to set `aria-pressed="true"` on the active period button and `aria-pressed="false"` on the inactive one(s).

issuesFound.title: Product filter results update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and grid content but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) || \`${totalMatches} products found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In setPeriod(), after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the error state of inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: In the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobile nav
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the page loads. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: In init(), after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for dropdown menus
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdown triggers but does not set aria-expanded="false" on them initially. Screen reader users may not know the dropdowns start collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: In init(), after registering each dropdown trigger in the loop, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons and switch do not set aria-pressed when toggled. Screen reader users cannot perceive the toggle state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In _syncToggleState, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive); for each period button and setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear); for the toggle switch.

issuesFound.title: product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: After updating the grid, add liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6000: In the invalid branch, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); in the valid branch, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: In the init function, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register function does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect initial state for dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: In the register function, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot know if the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: In the open function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2465: In the close function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot know if an answer is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3450: In the open function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3460: In the close function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause toggle button does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot know the current state (playing/paused) of the auto-play toggle, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In the play function, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` after `_playing = true;`. Line 4010: In the pause function, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` after `_playing = false;`. Line 4020: In togglePlay, ensure both play and pause are called.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not informed of the error state of the field, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: In _renderResult, inside the else block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 3610: In the valid block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In the register function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter applies new results without announcing the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the product grid updates, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: In applyFilters, after emitting the event, add a live region announcement: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || 'Filter applied.');`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that controls the dropdown menu in NavDropdown does not have aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is open or closed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the state of the disclosure, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1003: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` with a transition, but there is no corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule that disables or reduces this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 540: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform & box‑shadow)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-image img` applies a `transform` and `box‑shadow` transition, but the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty, so the animation is not suppressed for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 548: `.hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg); box-shadow: 0 32px 64px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.2); }`  
  Add: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; box-shadow: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle element (`_toggleEl` selected via `SELECTORS.billingToggle`) controls the display of monthly/annual pricing, yet the code never sets `aria-expanded` (or an equivalent state attribute) on this control. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the current state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Initialize with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` and toggle the attribute to `'true'` when the annual plan is shown and back to `'false'` when the monthly plan is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register`, each trigger button is given `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-controls`, but no `aria-expanded` attribute is set on load. The attribute is only added when the menu is opened via `_openInstance`. Without an initial `aria-expanded="false"` the button’s collapsed state is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `register`, after setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Update it to `'true'`/`'false'` in `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function updates visual classes (`fieldInvalid` / `fieldValid`) but never sets the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input element when validation fails, nor clears it when the field becomes valid. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive programmatic error information.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; when true, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')` (or set to `'false'`).

issuesFound.title: Toast dismiss button missing accessible name (if not already present)
issuesFound.explanation: The dismiss button created in `toastManager.show` has text content “×” but no `aria-label`. While the visual “×” may be unclear to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the dismiss button includes `aria-label` (e.g., `setAttr(dismissBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.dismissToast)`).  

(Note: Issue 6 is included as a precaution; if the button already receives an `aria-label` elsewhere, the fix is a no‑op.)

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` handlers in the `testimonialsCarousel` module (lines 11052–11100) are attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel container). These handlers implement swipe detection for touch/pointer devices, but they do not include matching keyboard handlers for Enter or Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe actions, effectively blocking access to carousel navigation.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module (lines 10220–10240) toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the open/closed state of the dropdown, blocking keyboard and assistive technology users from understanding the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button in both `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions.

Line 10220: Inside `_openInstance`, add:
```
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
```

Line 10230: Inside `_closeInstance`, add:
```
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module (lines 10800–10820) updates the displayed price and billing period label but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technology cannot perceive the updated pricing information.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an announcement to the shared live region immediately after updating the UI.

Line 10820: After the price updates, add:
```
const periodLabel = BILLING_PERIODS[period]?.label || period;
liveRegion.announce(`${STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to'} ${periodLabel}`);
```

issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (*) in the FormGroup component is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the asterisk, which is redundant since the "required" text is already conveyed via the VisuallyHidden component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1050: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Required field marked visually only — add aria-required or required attr
issuesFound.explanation: The "Full name" input in the ContactForm is marked as required visually with an asterisk, but the input element does not have the aria-required or required attribute. This prevents assistive technology from announcing that the field is required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2600: `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput used for the search field in the SearchInput component has an aria-label, but the label element is visually hidden and does not use htmlFor to associate it with the input. While the aria-label provides an accessible name, the visible label should be programmatically associated with the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1890: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" />`
  Fix: Line 1880: `<label id={labelId} htmlFor={id} className="site-search-form__label">`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not receive an aria-invalid prop when the invalid state is true. This prevents assistive technology from announcing that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size to 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. This results in body text rendering at 12px by default, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability, affecting users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: font-size: 100%;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia by making text appear crowded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 191: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Elements using this class will remain visible on the page, cluttering the interface and defeating the purpose of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify which button is focused, blocking form submission and interaction.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector uses #5588bb text on #ffffff background (inherited). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.83:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transition on transform, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 677) is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the animation.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector has a transition on transform, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. While some hover states add background colors, many links (e.g., in footer) rely solely on color changes to indicate interactivity, failing WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width and height to 28px with 0 padding. The total target size is 28px, which is below the recommended 44px and the minimum 24px for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width and height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement (such as box-shadow or border). This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users across the entire site, blocking navigation for those who do not use a mouse.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible is set to outline: none. This removes the focus indicator for users relying on high-contrast modes, making keyboard navigation impossible in that context.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it. This exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users and breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without consistent alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. While some components add hover states, links in general body text (e.g., inside paragraphs) rely solely on color to be distinguished from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The variable --colour-text-secondary is set to #aaaaaa. When used on the white page background (#ffffff), the contrast ratio is approximately 1.47:1, which is below the required 4.5:1 for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 26: --colour-text-secondary:   #767676;

issuesFound.title: Root font size set below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element has font-size: 12px. This makes the base font size (1rem) 12px, which is smaller than the recommended 16px for body text, reducing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213: font-size: 100%;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing reduces the space between words, which can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (Skip Link)
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform motion, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: (Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (Button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element uses transition: transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform motion, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 423: (Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (Hero Image)
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses transition: transform. The existing media query at Line 773 is empty and does not disable this motion, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 688: (Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small (Icon Button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels (SC 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small (Checkbox)
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width and height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels (SC 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1131: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (Button)
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (Hero Image)

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible focus style (such as box-shadow or border). This hides the focus state for all buttons using this class, failing WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Checkbox inputs rely on the browser default outline or a custom style to indicate focus; removing it hides keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1373: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Links in the footer navigation will not show a focus indicator, blocking keyboard access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only text and clutters the visual interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Live region content hidden visually
issuesFound.explanation: The [aria-live] rule hides all elements with aria-live attributes using clip and absolute positioning. This prevents users from seeing dynamic status updates (like form errors) that should be visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: [aria-live] { position: static; clip: auto; overflow: visible; width: auto; height: auto; white-space: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Base font size too small
issuesFound.explanation: The html element is set to font-size: 12px, making the base font size 12px (since body uses 1rem). This is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability and affects users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 127: html { font-size: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 151: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum (and 44px recommended) for touch targets. This makes it hard for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] is set to 20px width and height, below the 24px minimum. This makes it difficult to select on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button motion missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform for hover/active states without a reduced-motion override. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 for users who disable motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transition: transform for 3D effects. The existing media query at line 736 is empty, so the motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links lack underline on hover
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove text-decoration and only change color on hover. Color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1447: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Social links lack underline on hover
issuesFound.explanation: Social links remove text-decoration and only change color on hover. A non-color indicator (like underline) is required to meet WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1461: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced-motion media query block
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, providing no overrides for the many transitions defined in the stylesheet. It should disable animations globally or per-component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced-motion media query block for hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The media query for hero-image img is empty, failing to disable the transform transition defined at line 723 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

Button motion missing reduced-motion override
Hero image motion missing reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change, relying only on focus movement which may be disorienting.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line mobileNav.close(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown toggle buttons update the panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger. The initial state is also not set during registration. Screen reader users cannot perceive the open/closed state of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion open function updates the panel visibility and class but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers report "collapsed" even when expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not trigger a live region announcement. Users are not informed which question was expanded or collapsed, hindering navigation of the accordion content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || `${item.question} — answer expanded`);
  Line faqAccordion.close(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || `${item.question} — answer collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not manage aria-expanded for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion listbox. Showing or hiding the listbox does not update aria-expanded on the input. Screen reader users do not know if the suggestion list is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line suggestionListRenderer.show(): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line suggestionListRenderer.hide(): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the price display but does not announce the change to the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the prices have updated to reflect the new period.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not manage aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle the active visual state but do not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(): setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Applying a product filter updates the grid content but does not announce the change (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${s.activeCategory} products`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function adds an error description but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as valid, the function removes the error description but fails to remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". The field may remain announced as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid(): field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button moves the viewport but does not announce the action to the live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden change in position without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick(): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

fpTitles: productFilter changes are not announced to screen readers
scrollToTop action is not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggling the mobile navigation panel does not reflect its state via aria-expanded. Users cannot know if the menu is open or closed without visual cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons toggling sub-sections within the mobile navigation lack aria-expanded. The state of these nested disclosures is not announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This exposes visually hidden text to all users or fails to hide it properly, breaking the intended screen-reader-only behavior.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus ring from all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible for users who rely on visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a high-contrast alternative border or outline. This eliminates focus indicators for users in Windows High Contrast Mode, blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without underlines or other non-color visual differentiators, users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (line 385), causing spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition property, causing perspective rotation effects. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px reduces readability

Base font size below 16px reduces readability

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to assistive technologies. This contradicts the class's documented purpose and violates WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400-410: Replace with `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removal without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none globally without providing a replacement visual indicator (such as box-shadow or border) for interactive elements like buttons and links. While some components define their own focus styles, many inherit this removal and lack visible focus indicators, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 368-372: Add a replacement focus style such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or remove this global rule and apply outline removal only to specific components that provide alternative focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus outline removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none for :focus-visible without providing a replacement. In high contrast mode, outlines are essential for visibility, and their removal makes it impossible for keyboard users to determine which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380-384: Remove `outline: none;` from the forced-colors media query, or replace with `outline: 2px solid ButtonText;` to ensure visibility in high contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 603: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;` (or preferably 44px for AAA compliance)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 611: Change `min-width: 20px;` to `min-width: 24px;` and Line 612: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox selector sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: Change `width: 20px;` to `width: 24px;`, Line 1189: Change `height: 20px;` to `height: 24px;`, and Line 1190: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, failing to disable animations and transitions for users who prefer reduced motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 403-404: Add rules to disable motion, such as `* { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty, failing to disable the transform and transition effects that cause motion (perspective rotation on hover).
issuesFound.fix: Line 666-669: Add `.hero-image img { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; }` and `.hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; }` inside the media query.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 540), which can cause motion, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override in the file to disable this for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) or add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }` to the file.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making the default text size 12px (below 16px/1rem). This requires users to zoom to read comfortably and fails best practices for readable text.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menu triggers are registered, the panel is hidden with `setAttribute('hidden', '')`, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not initialized to "false". Screen readers may not convey the correct initial collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4505: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4265: Add `if (_drawer) { const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); }` after the lines assigning `_trigger` and `_drawer`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and focus is not trapped within the drawer while it is open. Keyboard users can tab outside the modal drawer, causing focus to be lost or to move behind the overlay.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4287: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` after `_lockScroll();`
  Line 4289: Add `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: firstFocusable }); _trap.activate();` to activate focus trapping

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3985: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked as valid, the function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the line that removes the aria-describedby attribute

issuesFound.title: pricing period change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users may not be aware that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6620: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + period + ' billing');` after the line `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results count missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the visible product count updates in the badge element, but this change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not be aware that the filter has been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6040: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` immediately after the line `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing focus trap activation and aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and locks scroll, but fails to activate the focus trap (which is also never initialized) and does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents keyboard users from being trapped in the drawer and screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (after `_lockScroll();`): `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: false });`
    Line (after previous): `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`
    Line (after previous): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing focus return and aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, and aria-expanded is not set to "false". This leaves keyboard users with lost focus and provides incorrect state information to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (after `_unlockScroll();`): `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`
    Line (after previous): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function missing focus return implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The function accepts a `returnFocus` parameter but never uses it to return focus to the trigger button. When users close the dropdown via Escape key, focus is lost instead of returning to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line (end of function, before emit): `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but lacks the required aria-expanded attribute to indicate whether the suggestions listbox is visible or hidden. This prevents screen readers from announcing the state changes when the listbox opens and closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in mount): `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`
    Line (in _open): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
    Line (in _close): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle uses role="switch" but does not update aria-checked to reflect the current state (monthly vs annual). Screen readers cannot announce whether the switch is currently on or off.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is visible but not announced to screen readers via a live region. Blind users are not informed that the filter action completed or how many results are showing.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not update its aria-expanded attribute when the menu opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce the state change to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line close function: Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (the close function sets it to "false", but the open function omits it). This leaves screen readers unaware that content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestion listbox opens or closes. This violates the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern which requires the input to indicate the popup state.
issuesFound.fix: Line _open: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line _close: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element has role="switch" but never updates aria-checked to reflect the current state (monthly vs annual). Screen readers cannot convey which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncToggleState: Add `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` after calculating `isYear` or directly using the period parameter.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter category buttons act as toggle buttons (one active at a time) but lack aria-pressed to indicate which filter is currently active, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand the current filter state.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncTabAriaStates: Inside the forEach loop, after `const isActive = ...`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period selection buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) do not indicate their selected state with aria-pressed, failing to communicate the current selection to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncToggleState: Inside `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { ... })`, after determining `isActive`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid: After `if (!isElement(field)) return;`, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav not focusing drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the navigation drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users remain on the toggle button and cannot access the menu content without tabbing through the entire page.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: After `_lockScroll();`, add `if (_drawer) { _drawer.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); _drawer.focus(); }` or `const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (first) first.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel slides missing explicit role
issuesFound.explanation: Slide elements have aria-roledescription="slide" but lack an explicit role attribute (should be role="group"). Some screen readers may not properly interpret the custom roledescription without a base role.
issuesFound.fix: Line init (slide setup): After `el.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'slide');`, add `el.setAttribute('role', 'group');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing results count
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the grid has updated or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters (end of filterAnimation.run callback): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing not announcing price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly/annual), the updated prices are not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize that costs have changed on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod: After `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);` in init, or at the end of the setPeriod function, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing`);`.

fpTitles: mobileNav not focusing drawer on open
testimonialsCarousel slides missing explicit role

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing button semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div in MobileNav has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate this element to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2240: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation" className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses mobile navigation sections has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing the current expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls pointing to the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded, so assistive technology cannot announce whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an emoji icon (sun/moon/computer) without an aria-label, leaving screen reader users without any indication of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2340: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous slide button contains only the "‹" character and lacks an aria-label, so screen reader users hear only "less than" or silence with no context that it navigates to the previous slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next slide button contains only the "›" character and lacks an aria-label, so screen reader users hear only "greater than" with no indication it navigates to the next slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3555: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to style the field as erroneous but does not map this to aria-invalid, so screen reader users are not notified when a field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820-1827: `export const TextInput: FC<TextInputProps> = ({ id, invalid, className, ...rest }) => ( <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} /> );`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy attribute during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an `isLoading` prop to show a spinner and disable the button but does not set aria-busy, so screen reader users are not informed that the button is processing and unavailable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560-1580: Add `aria-busy={isLoading}` to the button element: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a CSS class for visual styling but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicating the current page uses a CSS class for visual styling but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a `<nav>` element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish this landmark from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a `<nav>` element with an id but no aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2110: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component contains a `<nav>` element inside the dialog that lacks an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the purpose of this navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2270: `<nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: StatItem not exposing screen-reader-friendly value label
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component renders the formatted value (e.g., "40,000+") but ignores the `valueLabel` property (e.g., "40,000 plus") provided in the data, causing screen readers to announce confusing punctuation like "forty thousand plus" instead of the intended text alternative.

Line 2650: `

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class does not hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — these properties make content VISIBLE rather than hiding it.
