issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. With padding of approximately 10.5px top/bottom, the total touch target is approximately 41px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44×44px for pointer inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 471-476: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. With padding of approximately 7.5px on each side, the total touch target is approximately 35px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44×44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 484-488: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px with no padding, creating a 20×20px touch target. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44×44px (or 24×24px minimum with spacing).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); margin: 10px; }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 682-687 is empty and does not disable the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 682-687: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class has a transform transition and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 456: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Footer navigation links focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
Table wrapper focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not move focus into drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not programmatically moved into the drawer. Keyboard users must manually Tab into the menu, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place on the page, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true". Screen reader users are not notified of the invalid state through accessible properties.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field becomes valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not cleared. Screen reader users may continue to hear the field announced as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the visible count changes but screen reader users are not notified of the dynamic content change. The count badge update is purely visual.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, prices update visually but screen reader users are not notified of the change. Users may not realize the prices have changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS classes on billing period buttons but never sets aria-pressed="true"/"false" to indicate the active state. Toggle buttons must expose their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4401: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1730: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the error is cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby cleanup block.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets aria-haspopup on the trigger but never initializes aria-expanded="false". The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3061: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the aria-haspopup assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the null check for _trigger and _drawer.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the visible product count changes, there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3868: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(activeCategory) || 'Products filtered');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle changes the displayed prices, there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4365: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function accepts a returnFocus parameter but never uses it to restore focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place when the dropdown closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3043: add `if (returnFocus) { inst.trigger.focus(); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is never created
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares `_trap = null` and references it in close() but never creates a focus trap with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not trapped within it, allowing keyboard users to escape into the page content behind the overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2862: add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();` after the _lockScroll() call in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation drawer has opened, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2889: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes and unlocks scroll but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation drawer has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2901: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares `let _trap = null;` and references it in close() (`if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`), but never creates or activates a focus trap in open(). When the mobile drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined within it, allowing users to Tab outside the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2889: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown, the function adds the hidden attribute and removes CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3009: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never initialises aria-expanded="false". The initial state is hidden/closed, so assistive technologies need to know the starting state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3066: `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open = true, adds CSS classes, and removes the hidden attribute, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4113: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function only toggles a CSS active class on filter tabs but never sets aria-pressed. Filter tabs function as toggle buttons (one active at a time), so screen reader users need to know which filter is currently pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3570: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle lacks aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS classes on billing period buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Users cannot programmatically determine which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3955: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validated fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function adds/removes CSS validity classes and links fields to error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4594: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` and Line 4599: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for closing the mobile navigation menu has no aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons, but it is not passed here, leaving screen reader users with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing IconButton: `<IconButton aria-label="Close menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next carousel buttons are IconButtons containing only arrow characters (‹ and ›) with no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot understand the purpose of these buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line containing prev button: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`
  Fix: Line containing next button: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page uses className to indicate active state but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing the anchor: `<a ... aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Duplicate content not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The second `<ul>` element is a visual clone for seamless looping but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the integration list twice, creating confusing duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing the cloned ul: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple navigation landmarks exist on the page. This nav element lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish between navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing nav: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The button component has an isLoading state but does not communicate this to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed when a button is in a loading/busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing the button: `<button ... aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk used to indicate required fields has no aria-hidden attribute. Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "*" which is not meaningful to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing the span: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Statistic value lacks accessible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value displays formatted strings like "4.2M" which may be announced ambiguously by screen readers. The valueLabel property (e.g., "4.2 million") is defined but not used to provide a clearer alternative.
issuesFound.fix:
Line containing the paragraph: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay container div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This may cause inconsistent behavior with assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line containing modal-overlay: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line containing dialog div: `<div ... role="dialog" aria-modal="true" ...>`

issuesFound.title: Decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron arrow character indicating dropdown state is decorative (the state should be conveyed via aria-expanded) but lacks aria-hidden="true", causing redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing the span: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The LABELS object is defined but never used. Screen reader users receive no accessible name for this button, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the modal-dialog div. Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the element with role="dialog". This causes screen readers to incorrectly announce the modal's boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is visible but not hidden from screen readers. Since a visually hidden "(required)" text is already provided, the asterisk creates redundant announcement ("* (required)" instead of just "(required)").
issuesFound.fix: Line ~420: Add aria-hidden="true" to the asterisk span: ```tsx * ```

issuesFound.title: Trust badge image has redundant alt text
issuesFound.explanation: The WCAG badge image has alt="WCAG 2.2 AA compliant" but is adjacent to visible text "WCAG 2.2 AA". Screen readers announce "WCAG 2.2 AA compliant WCAG 2.2 AA" which is redundant. The image is decorative since the text conveys the information.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~900: Change the alt attribute to empty: ```tsx WCAG 2.2 AA ```

issuesFound.title: Integration link image has redundant alt text
issuesFound.explanation: Each integration link contains an image with alt text like "React integration" adjacent to visible text "React". Screen readers announce "React integration React" which is redundant. The image is decorative since the text already identifies the integration.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~975: Change the alt attribute to empty: ```tsx ```

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks navigation element has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation from others when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~380: Add aria-label to the nav element:
```tsx
<nav aria-label="Skip links">
```

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav navigation element has an id but no aria-label. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation from others when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~680: Add aria-label to the nav element:
```tsx
<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">
```

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden for non-visible slides
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible are not hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users can navigate to all slides regardless of visibility, creating confusion about which content is actually displayed.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input's aria-describedby references "newsletter-email-error" but no element with that ID exists in the form. The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props but receives an error prop instead. Screen readers cannot announce the error message when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1800: Pass the correct props to FormGroup and ensure error message element exists:
```tsx
<FormGroup
  label="Email address"
  htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  errorId="newsletter-email-error"
  errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  required
>
  <TextInput
    id="newsletter-email"
    type="email"
    name="email"
    placeholder="you@company.com"
    autoComplete="email"
    value={email.state.value}
    onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
    onBlur={email.onBlur}
    required
    invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
    aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
  />
</FormGroup>
```

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: Each form input in ContactForm references an error message ID (e.g., "contact-name-error") via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component receives an error prop instead of errorId and errorMessage. The error message elements do not exist, so screen readers cannot announce validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1700: Pass the correct props to each FormGroup:
```tsx
<FormGroup
  label="Full name"
  htmlFor="contact-name"
  errorId="contact-name-error"
  errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  required
>
  <TextInput
    id="contact-name"
    name="name"
    type="text"
    autoComplete="name"
    required
    aria-required="true"
    value={name.state.value}
    onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
    onBlur={name.onBlur}
    invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
    aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
  />
</FormGroup>
```
Apply the same pattern for email, subject, and message fields with their respective errorId and errorMessage props.

fpTitles: Trust badge image has redundant alt text

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button controls a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate its open/closed state to screen readers. Users cannot tell whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls the mobile nav panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the menu is open or closed to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav submenu trigger buttons control expandable sections but lack aria-expanded to communicate their state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate its open/closed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle buttons indicate selection state through visual styling but lack aria-pressed to communicate which option is currently selected to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state through visual styling but lack aria-pressed to communicate which filter is currently selected to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle IconButton renders without an aria-label prop, leaving the button without an accessible name. The LABELS object is defined but never passed to the component.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for closing the mobile navigation lacks the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without a way to identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 830: <IconButton
  onClick={onClose}
  aria-label="Close navigation menu"
  className="mobile-nav__close"
>
  ✕
</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next IconButtons in the testimonials carousel lack the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without a way to identify these navigation buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1418: <IconButton
  aria-label="Previous testimonial"
  aria-controls={trackId}
  onClick={handlePrev}
  className="testimonials-track__prev"
>
  ‹
</IconButton>
Line 1424: <IconButton
  aria-label="Next testimonial"
  aria-controls={trackId}
  onClick={handleNext}
  className="testimonials-track__next"
>
  ›
</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Current navigation links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Desktop navigation links marked as current via className lack aria-current="page" to programmatically indicate the current page to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Current mobile nav links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links marked as current via className lack aria-current="page" to programmatically indicate the current page to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div. aria-modal must be on the element with role="dialog" to properly signal modality to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Badge content hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card badge containing "Most popular" has aria-hidden="true", hiding important informational content from screen reader users. This content should be accessible.

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative element not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner's inner ring element is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". The parent span already provides the accessible name via aria-label, so the ring should be hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: {label}

issuesFound.title: Stat value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value displays formatted numbers like "40,000+" or "4.2M" without providing a screen-reader-friendly alternative. The valueLabel property exists in the Stat interface but is never used.
